preamble: The shift from 'ɡʲ' to 'ʒ' mirrors the voiceless counterpart (kʲ → ʃ), involving the spirantization of a voiced palatalized velar stop. This transformation frequently passes through a voiced palato-alveolar affricate stage [dʒ].

examples.note: Historical French evolution where Latin /ɡ/ before /a/ became /dʒ/ and later /ʒ/ (e.g., Latin 'gambam' > French 'jambe').
Slavic First Palatalization where voiced velars became postalveolar fricatives.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
